rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in component type scopes for assumptions and guarantees that reference component ports.
Use implementation scopes for bindings and assignments that are valid in that context.
generic_rules: Ensure AGREE contracts are placed in valid AADL scopes based on whether they reference component ports or implementation details.
Only include assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes when they refer to visible ports or features.
Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type contracts.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Constants and equations in AGREE should be declared with appropriate types matching AADL port data types.
anti_patterns: Avoid placing implementation-level bindings in component type scopes unless the feature is explicitly represented in the type.
Do not assume numeric bounds for ports unless explicitly stated in the requirement or compatible with the AADL data type.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE syntax and annex placement rules
